items.text: Profile parser accepts markdown files with YAML frontmatter and extracts structured fields plus markdown body as system prompt.
Profile frontmatter supports name, description, model, tools (list or comma-separated), disallowedTools, reasoning_effort, reasoning_summary, and capabilities fields.
Profiles resolve from ~/.norn/profiles/ as .md files.
Two-tier scanning: workspace-level ({cwd}/.norn/profiles/) checked first, user-level (~/.norn/profiles/) as fallback. Workspace wins on name collision.
Meridian profiles at {cwd}/.meridian/profiles/ are checked as an additional scan tier.
Profile resolution is available from libnorn (not CLI-only) so SpawnAgentTool can load profiles at runtime.
norn-cli profile_loader.rs delegates to libnorn's profile loader instead of its own TOML/JSON parser.
paths.rs profiles_dir() returns ~/.norn/profiles/ not ~/.norn/config/profiles/.
Capability parser accepts markdown files with YAML frontmatter and extracts tools, disallowedTools, and prompt_fragment.
Capability resolution merges capabilities into a profile: tools union, disallowedTools append, prompt_fragment appends to system prompt.
DiskTaskStore persists tasks to ~/.norn/tasks/{group-slug}/ as individual {task-id}.json files. Task groups are session-agnostic.
DiskTaskStore implements the existing TaskStore trait with all five operations (create, get, list, update, complete) plus create_group, list_groups.
TaskEntry has parent_task_id: Option<String> for hierarchical task trees.
TaskEntry has assigned_agent: Option<String> recording which agent (by registry path) is assigned to the task.
create_subtask creates a task with parent_task_id set, forming a tree.
children(parent_id) returns direct child tasks of a parent.
ancestors(task_id) walks the parent chain to the root task.
Status roll-up: parent effective status is Blocked if any child Blocked, InProgress if any child InProgress, Completed only when all children Completed.
claim(task_id, agent_path) atomically sets assigned_agent only if currently unassigned.
SharedTaskStore installed on ToolContext during build_runtime.
InMemoryTaskStore remains available for testing and ephemeral sessions.
AgentToolInfra installed on ToolContext during build_runtime with AgentRegistry, Mailbox, Provider, EventStore, root agent UUID, and ToolRegistry.
SharedToolCatalog installed on ToolContext during build_runtime, built from the registry's tool definitions.
All five agent tools (spawn_agent, fork, send_message, wait_agent, close_agent) return successful results when invoked, not extension-missing errors.
task tool returns successful results when invoked, not extension-missing errors.
tool_search tool returns successful results when invoked, not extension-missing errors.
SpawnAgentTool launches the child via tokio::spawn and returns immediately with agent_id and registry path.
SpawnAgentTool accepts an optional profile parameter and builds the child LoopContext via from_profile() when provided.
Child agent receives filtered tool definitions from SubAgentExecutor's allow-list, not an empty slice.
AgentHandle stored as ToolContext extension keyed by agent_id, providing status watch receiver and InboundSender.
InboundChannel created for each child agent; parent holds the sender via AgentHandle.
Child agent drains InboundChannel at tool boundaries using the existing Steer/FollowUp drain logic.
WaitAgentTool uses tokio::sync::watch channel subscription instead of 50ms polling.
WaitAgentTool supports waiting on multiple agents via tokio::select! for first-to-finish.
Child sends completion notification to parent's mailbox with trigger_turn: true on reaching terminal status.
close_agent performs DFS shutdown of all descendants before transitioning the target.
Sub-agents can be configured with EventType::Progress schema via EventSchemaSet for structured status updates.
tool_use_description on sub-agent tool calls is accessible to the parent for progress visibility.
Sub-agent's EventStore is linked to the parent's session for audit trail purposes.
